title: 1.5 Billion Dollar Settlement Approved by Judge: Anthropic Copyright Case Concludes, Ending Writer's Class-Action Lawsuit
excerpt: A copyright battle that has captured the entire AI industry has finally reached a conclusion with the judge's gavel. According to a report by Jiemian News, on J
content_html:
A copyright battle that has captured the entire AI industry has finally reached a conclusion with the judge's gavel. According to a report by Jiemian News, on July 20 local time, US Federal Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín in the Northern District of California officially approved a $1.5 billion settlement agreement involving the AI company Anthropic and rejected objections regarding the low compensation amount. The agreement aims to resolve a class-action lawsuit filed by a group of writers—this group had accused Anthropic as early as 2024 of using their works without permission to train its AI chatbot Claude.

The judge's approval marks the end of this landmark confrontation over the ownership of training data copyrights, and also adds a significant precedent for the legal boundaries of AI companies using copyrighted texts as training material.

According to a report by Jiemian News, on July 20 local time, US Federal Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín in the Northern District of California officially approved a $1.5 billion settlement agreement involving the AI company Anthropic and rejected objections regarding the low compensation amount. The agreement aims to resolve a class-action lawsuit filed by a group of writers—this group had accused Anthropic as early as 2024 of using their works without permission to train its AI chatbot Claude.The judge's approval marks the end of this landmark confrontation over the ownership of training data copyrights, and also adds a significant precedent for the legal boundaries of AI companies using copyrighted texts as training material.

blocks.html_en: A copyright battle that has captured the entire AI industry has finally reached a conclusion with the judge's gavel. According to a report by Jiemian News, on July 20 local time, US Federal Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín in the Northern District of California officially approved a $1.5 billion settlement agreement involving the AI company Anthropic and rejected objections regarding the low compensation amount. The agreement aims to resolve a class-action lawsuit filed by a group of writers—this group had accused Anthropic as early as 2024 of using their works without permission to train its AI chatbot Claude.

sentences.en: A copyright battle that has captured the entire AI industry has finally reached a conclusion with the judge's gavel.
sentences.zh: 一场席卷整个AI行业的版权之争，终于随着法官的木槌落下帷幕。

sentences.en: According to a report by Jiemian News, on July 20 local time, US Federal Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín in the Northern District of California officially approved a $1.5 billion settlement agreement involving the AI company Anthropic and rejected objections regarding the low compensation amount.
sentences.zh: 据界面新闻报道，当地时间7月20日，美国加州北区联邦法官阿拉塞利·马丁内斯-奥尔金正式批准涉及人工智能公司Anthropic的15亿美元和解协议，并驳回了有关赔偿金额过低的异议。

sentences.en: The agreement aims to resolve a class-action lawsuit filed by a group of writers—this group had accused Anthropic as early as 2024 of using their works without permission to train its AI chatbot Claude.
sentences.zh: 该协议旨在解决一群作家提起的集体诉讼——该组织早在 2024 年就指控 Anthropic 在未经许可的情况下使用他们的作品来训练其人工智能聊天机器人 Claude。

blocks.html_en: The judge's approval marks the end of this landmark confrontation over the ownership of training data copyrights, and also adds a significant precedent for the legal boundaries of AI companies using copyrighted texts as training material.

sentences.en: The judge's approval marks the end of this landmark confrontation over the ownership of training data copyrights, and also adds a significant precedent for the legal boundaries of AI companies using copyrighted texts as training material.
sentences.zh: 法官的批准标志着这场关于训练数据版权归属的里程碑式对抗的结束，也为人工智能公司使用受版权保护的文本作为训练材料的法律边界增添了一个重要先例。

content_html_zh:
一场席卷整个AI行业的版权之争，终于随着法官的木槌落下帷幕。 据界面新闻报道，当地时间7月20日，美国加州北区联邦法官阿拉塞利·马丁内斯-奥尔金正式批准涉及人工智能公司Anthropic的15亿美元和解协议，并驳回了有关赔偿金额过低的异议。 该协议旨在解决一群作家提起的集体诉讼——该组织早在 2024 年就指控 Anthropic 在未经许可的情况下使用他们的作品来训练其人工智能聊天机器人 Claude。

法官的批准标志着这场关于训练数据版权归属的里程碑式对抗的结束，也为人工智能公司使用受版权保护的文本作为训练材料的法律边界增添了一个重要先例。
